req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.
preconditions: source.owner == target.owner

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three key behavioural axes: (1) normal transfer within daily limit, (2) transfer exceeding daily limit, (3) exact boundary at $25,000, and (4) idempotency via Idempotency-Key. EP covers the valid transfer partition; BVA covers the $25,000 boundary; NEG covers the over-limit decline and insufficient-funds paths; DT covers the idempotency contract; STC covers the account state after a successful transfer. Each test uses unique, pre-seeded fixture accounts to remain self-contained and deterministic.

tests.name: test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $1,000
Daily transfer total for customer is $0
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=$1000, unique Idempotency-Key, valid source/target account IDs
expected: Response body contains transfer id, status=COMPLETED
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds(api_client, seeded_accounts):
    src, tgt = seeded_accounts(balance=5000)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src["id"],
        "target_account_id": tgt["id"],
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "COMPLETED"
    assert "id" in body

    src_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src['id']}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt['id']}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_balance == 4000
    assert tgt_balance == 1000

tests.name: test_transfer_exceeding_daily_limit_is_declined
preconditions: Customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $25,001
Daily transfer total for customer is $0
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=$25,001, unique Idempotency-Key, valid source/target account IDs
expected: Response body error code indicates DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
trace: R-FIN-078: transfers above $25,000 shall be declined
R-FIN-078: error pathway 422 when daily limit exceeded
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_transfer_exceeding_daily_limit_is_declined(api_client, seeded_accounts):
    src, tgt = seeded_accounts(balance=30000)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src["id"],
        "target_account_id": tgt["id"],
        "amount": 25001,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"

    src_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src['id']}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_balance == 30000

tests.name: test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_boundary_succeeds
preconditions: Customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $25,000
Daily transfer total for customer is $0
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=$25,000, unique Idempotency-Key, valid source/target account IDs
trace: R-FIN-078: customer shall transfer UP TO $25,000 per day (inclusive boundary)
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_boundary_succeeds(api_client, seeded_accounts):
    src, tgt = seeded_accounts(balance=25000)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src["id"],
        "target_account_id": tgt["id"],
        "amount": 25000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "COMPLETED"

    src_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src['id']}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt['id']}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_balance == 0
    assert tgt_balance == 25000

tests.name: test_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result
preconditions: Customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $500
Daily transfer total for customer is $0
actions: POST /transfers/internal again with identical payload and same Idempotency-Key=KEY-IDEM-001
expected: First response: HTTP 201, status=COMPLETED, transfer id=T1
Second response: HTTP 200 (or 201), same transfer id=T1, same status=COMPLETED
Source balance decremented only once by $500 (not twice)
trace: R-FIN-078: idempotency contract - duplicate Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result(api_client, seeded_accounts):
    src, tgt = seeded_accounts(balance=2000)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src["id"],
        "target_account_id": tgt["id"],
        "amount": 500,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp1 = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code in (200, 201)
    body1 = resp1.json()

    resp2 = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code in (200, 201)
    body2 = resp2.json()

    assert body1["id"] == body2["id"]
    assert body2["status"] == body1["status"]

    src_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src['id']}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_balance == 1500

tests.name: test_transfer_declined_on_insufficient_funds
preconditions: Customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance = $100
Daily transfer total for customer is $0
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=$500, unique Idempotency-Key, valid source/target account IDs
expected: Response body error code indicates INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
trace: R-FIN-078: error pathway 409 on insufficient funds
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_transfer_declined_on_insufficient_funds(api_client, seeded_accounts):
    src, tgt = seeded_accounts(balance=100)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src["id"],
        "target_account_id": tgt["id"],
        "amount": 500,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"

    src_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src['id']}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_balance == 100

tests.name: test_account_state_transitions_after_successful_transfer
preconditions: Customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account status=ACTIVE, balance=$2,000
Target account status=ACTIVE, balance=$0
Daily transfer total for customer is $0
actions: Record initial balances of source and target
Poll transfer status until terminal state (COMPLETED or FAILED)
trace: R-FIN-078: expected outcome - funds moved atomically with observable state transitions
tests.executable:
import uuid
import time
import pytest

def test_account_state_transitions_after_successful_transfer(api_client, seeded_accounts):
    src, tgt = seeded_accounts(balance=2000)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src["id"],
        "target_account_id": tgt["id"],
        "amount": 1500,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    transfer_id = resp.json()["id"]

    final_status = poll_until_terminal(api_client, transfer_id)
    assert final_status == "COMPLETED"

    src_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src['id']}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_balance = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt['id']}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_balance == 500
    assert tgt_balance == 1500
